title: [Feature]: Add validated headers-only JetStream consumer configuration
problem: NATS JetStream supports headers-only consumers, but nats-sinks does not expose a validated way to request, verify, or reconcile that setting. Operators can create such consumers manually, but nats-sinks cannot currently prove that the durable consumer is intentionally metadata-only or fail closed when an existing consumer has incompatible delivery behavior.
proposal: Add explicit configuration for headers-only consumer operation under the future JetStream consumer management model. The setting must be disabled by default, validated before message processing, and reconciled with existing durable consumers only through documented bind-only, create-if-missing, or reconcile modes. The runner should require explicit operator intent before accepting a headers-only consumer and should produce a clear configuration error when consumer state conflicts with the configured mode.
users: Operators who need metadata-only custody workflows, maintainers improving NATS feature compatibility, and security reviewers who need payload minimization to be explicit and auditable.
delivery_semantics: Headers-only operation must preserve pull-based processing and commit-then-acknowledge semantics. ACK must still happen only after the metadata-only sink write or DLQ publication has durably succeeded.
security: The feature reduces delivered payload exposure but metadata remains sensitive. Configuration must fail closed, avoid broad NATS management permissions by default, and document the difference between bind-only runtime permissions and administrative consumer reconciliation permissions.
acceptance: Headers-only consumer mode is explicit and disabled by default.
Existing consumer state is verified before processing starts.
Incompatible consumer state fails closed with a clear error.
Runtime and administrative NATS permissions are documented separately.
Tests cover bind-only, create-if-missing, compatible existing consumer, and incompatible drift.
tests: Add unit tests with mocked JetStream management APIs and optional local NATS integration tests for a headers-only durable consumer. Run scripts/check.sh and targeted NATS consumer tests.
documentation: Update the NATS documentation tree, docs/configuration.md, docs/architecture.md, docs/security.md, docs/headers-only-delivery.md, ROADMAP.md, and CHANGELOG.md with configuration, permissions, failure behavior, and migration guidance.
closeout: Close after the release containing validated headers-only consumer configuration is published. Include release tag, tested consumer modes, permission notes, and limitations.
